module_map.responsibility: Orchestrates the entire pipeline: initializes Colors/Icons/GitTheme, fetches metadata for all paths, sorts results, and dispatches to display (grid or tree mode). Bridges CLI flags to execution logic.

Collects filesystem metadata for a single file/directory. Constructs Meta struct by querying OS metadata, resolving symlinks, extracting file type, permissions, size, dates, owner. Recursively populates nested directory content.
Renders Meta entries to terminal output. Routes to grid or tree layout. Applies blocks (columns), detects terminal width, applies padding/alignment, inserts headers, handles hyperlinks and colors.

Defines and parses CLI arguments and config file options. Contains per-feature flag parsers (blocks, colors, icons, sorting, permissions, recursion, etc.). Provides type-safe enum representations of all user-configurable options.
Maps file type, Git status, or UI element to terminal color/style. Manages color themes and applies ANSI escape codes. Wraps strings in colored output.
Maps file name, extension, or type to a visual icon. Integrates icon themes. Conditionally renders icons based on TTY and icon flags.
Provides theme data: color palette definitions and icon mappings. Loads from built-in themes or external files.
Queries git repository for file status and decorations. Caches results per directory to avoid repeated git calls.
Implements sorting logic for Meta entries by name, size, date, type, or git status. Chains multiple sort criteria in defined order.

types_produced: Meta{path, name, file_type, size, date, owner, permissions_or_attributes, symlink, access_control, indicator, inode, links}
data_flow.description: Calls fs::symlink_metadata() → checks symlink status → collects Owner, Permissions, FileType, Size, Date, AccessControl via type-specific extractors (e.g., Owner::from(&metadata), Size::from(&metadata)). Returns fully populated Meta struct.
Iterates directory entries, calls Meta::from_path() for each, optionally applies git status lookup. Builds nested Vec<Meta> for directory contents. Depth controls recursion limit.
For each SortOrder in flags (e.g., SortColumn::Name, SortColumn::Size), applies comparator via SortFn closure chain. Recursively sorts nested content. No new types, in-place sort.

Grid: calls inner_display_grid() → for each Meta, calls get_output() to build per-block strings (name, size, date, perms, owner, git status). Constructs term_grid::Grid with Cell entries (width, contents). Fits to terminal width or column count. Tree: adds tree prefix characters (├──, └──, │) and indentation.

For each Block in flags.blocks (Name, Size, Date, Permissions, Owner, GitStatus, INode, Links), calls meta::<TYPE>::render(colors, flags). Icon resolution via Icons::new() → icon::get(file_type, name). Color resolution via Colors::style(elem). Returns Vec of colored block strings.

Icon: looks up FileType, file extension, or name in IconTheme map. Color: maps Elem (enum: File, Dir, Link, etc.) to ColorTheme entry, applies color code via crossterm::style. Both consult theme/ for definitions.
Macro writes String from stage 4 to stdout. Includes all ANSI escape sequences for colors, styles, hyperlinks.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Core imports Meta. Display imports Meta, FileType, Name, OwnerCache. Sort imports Meta. Git imports GitFileStatus. Icon imports FileType, Name. meta/ only imports Flags (flags/) and Colors (color/). Central hub because all rendering and sorting flows through the Meta struct produced here.

Core calls display::grid() or display::tree(). Display imports Meta, Flags, Colors, Icons, GitTheme, and internal types (Cell, Grid, OwnerCache). Display is the final rendering stage that consumes all collected metadata and styling.

Core imports Flags and specific flag enums. Meta imports Flags (for permission, display, layout decisions). Display imports Flags. Sort imports Flags and SortColumn/SortOrder. Flags is a configuration hub: no outbound crate::* dependencies, only standard library and external crates. All modules depend on it for behavior control.

change_proposal.goal: Add a file checksum display column (SHA-256)
files_to_modify: src/meta/checksum.rs (new file; define Checksum type and SHA-256 computation)
src/color.rs (optional; define color mapping for Checksum elem if needed)
new_types: meta::checksum::Checksum struct (holds hash as [u8; 32] or hex string)
meta::checksum::ChecksumError enum (for I/O errors during hashing)
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow meta/size.rs pattern: (1) Define struct with bytes/data field. (2) Impl From<&Metadata> to extract from OS metadata (or manually compute from file content). (3) Define render(colors: &Colors, flags: &Flags) -> ColoredString method. (4) Integrate into Meta struct initialization in from_path() (line 337-348). (5) Add to Block enum and handle in get_output() switch/match.
change_proposal.integration_point: Meta::from_path() collects Checksum::from(&metadata) or Checksum::from_path(&path) [requires file read]. Display::get_output() adds checksum block to blocks vec. User enables via --blocks name,checksum flag. Color.rs applies color::Elem::Checksum styling. Theme files can define checksum color (optional).
implementation_notes: Checksum computation is I/O-bound; consider sha2 or blake3 crate dependency.
Decide: compute on-demand in render() or cache in Meta struct at from_path(). Caching is safer; from_path() should compute once.
Handle broken symlinks: set Checksum to None if file cannot be read (consistent with other optional fields like Date for broken links, line 339).
Performance: lazy-compute checksums only if Block::Checksum is in flags.blocks (check before calling compute in from_path). This avoids hashing every file if checksum column not requested.
Formatting: display hex string (e.g., 'a1b2c3d4...' truncated to 12 chars) with optional full-length option in flags/checksum.rs.
risks: Performance: SHA-256 hashing every file on large directories is slow. Mitigation: lazy-compute only when Block::Checksum is requested.
File access: from_path() already reads file for size/date; hashing adds second full read. Mitigation: cache the hash computation result in Checksum struct.
Broken symlinks/inaccessible files: Checksum::from_path() must handle errors gracefully (set to None, consistent with Size for broken links).
Column width: SHA-256 hex (64 chars) is wide; truncation or full display option needed. Add ChecksumFlag to flags/checksum.rs (Full/Short/Disabled).
Dependency creep: adding sha2 or blake3 crate; ensure no duplicate hashing deps.
Tree mode recursion: if recursing into many directories, checksum computation multiplies. Consider opt-in flag to disable checksums in tree mode.
Git cache interaction: Checksum not related to git status, but file modification changes checksum. No interaction risk, but document assumption that checksum reflects file-at-read-time.

tool_usage.params: max_depth=2 on src/ to get overview of 52 files, 13223 LOC, 672 functions, 171 classes (modules)
max_depth=0 on display.rs, core.rs, meta/mod.rs to extract function names, imports, and type references

focus=from_path, follow_depth=2 on meta/mod.rs to trace metadata extraction: 43 outgoing calls (symlink_metadata, Owner::from, Permissions::from, FileType::new, Name::new, Size::from, Date::from, etc.)
focus=grid, follow_depth=2 on src/ to trace grid rendering: 31 outgoing calls via inner_display_grid to Grid, Cell, get_output, get_visible_width, add_header
view core.rs to understand Core::run → fetch → sort → display pipeline
view meta/mod.rs lines 1-100 to understand Meta struct fields and submodule organization
view meta/mod.rs lines 262-365 to trace from_path implementation and type construction
view display.rs lines 1-250 to understand grid/tree entry points and get_output block rendering
view meta/size.rs lines 1-80 as pattern reference for new Checksum type (Size::from(&metadata), render(colors, flags) -> ColoredString)
rg '^use crate::' src/**/*.rs to identify cross-module import patterns and dependency hubs
